title: trichasterophyllum majus
side_effects: Trichasterophyllum majus can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues. Ingesting the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin rashes, blisters, and itching, while eye contact may result in redness, swelling, and blurred vision. Improper use of the plant can also cause respiratory problems, such as coughing and shortness of breath, in individuals with pre-existing conditions.
